zh.lifeSummary: 當Marthe Roger de Beaufort於1335年出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰可雷洛希艾斯-達艾格萊頓斯時，她的父親Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT41歲，母親Marie de CHAMBON42歲。她於1353年12月17日與Guy II de la Tour結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1379年9月6日在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯洛易蒙特布利森享年44歲，埋葬在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯洛易蒙特布利森。

zh-hans.Olliergues, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-: 法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o奧利格斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Marthe Roger de Beaufort于1335年出生在法国努維勒-阿吉泰可雷洛希艾斯-達艾格萊頓斯时，她的父亲Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT41岁，母亲Marie de CHAMBON42岁。她于1353年12月17日与Guy II de la Tour结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1379年9月6日在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯洛易蒙特布利森享年44岁，埋葬在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯洛易蒙特布利森。

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rogero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Cayetano, Jose, Silva.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogero (see Roger ).
Catalan (Rogeró): from a pet form of the personal name Roger .

variants.name: Rieck
meanings: South German: from a pet form of the personal name Ru(o)diger, a compound of Old High German hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + gēr ‘spear, lance’ (see Roger ).
North German: from a short form of a personal name containing the element rīc ‘power(ful)’.
Germanized form of Sorbian Rjek: nickname from Upper Sorbian rjek ‘hero’. Compare Reck .

variants.name: Rogelio
meanings: Spanish; Portuguese (Rogélio): from the personal name Rogelio, Portuguese Rogélio, of ancient Germanic origin (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Roger  .

variants.name: Rogers
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Roger , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Ruaidhrí (see Rorie ).

History: Thomas Rogers (c. 1587–1621), born in London, England, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He died during the first winter at Plymouth Colony, but his son Joseph survived and married, and was later joined in MA by his brother John. This name was subsequently brought to North America independently by many different bearers.

variants.name: Rogel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Jose Luis, Juan, Marcos, Margarita, Wilfredo, Adilia, Alejandro, Ana Gladys, Ana Maria, Anacleto.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogel, an equivalent of French Roger .

Americanized form of Slovenian Rogelj: nickname or topographic name from rogelj ‘horn, point’, figuratively ‘tall young man’, as a topographic name probably denoting someone who lived e.g. on a horn-shaped spur of a hill. The surname in the form Rogel is found in Slovenia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Prosser
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Rhosier ‘son of Roger’ (see Roger and Rosser ).

variants.name: Rogerson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Roger + -son.

variants.name: Ritger
meanings: Altered form of German and Dutch Rutger or Rütger, from the personal name Rudiger (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rogge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Elke, Hans, Helmut, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Roger  .
North German and Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a grower of rye or a baker of rye bread, from Low German, Dutch rogge ‘rye’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaufort
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French   Beaufort  .
English (of Norman origin): variant of Beaufort . The surname Beauford is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beuford
meanings: Variant of   Beauford  .

nameMeanings.name: Marthe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament. See Martha .

variants.name: Marta
meanings: contracted form of Märeta, an obsolete variant of Merete . The name is also popular in Sweden.

variants.name: Marte
meanings: New Testament name, of Aramaic rather than Hebrew origin, meaning ‘lady’. It was borne by the sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (John 11:1). According to Luke 10:38, when Jesus visited the house of Mary and Martha, Mary sat at his feet, listening to him, while Martha ‘was cumbered about much serving’, so that she complained to Jesus, ‘Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone?’ For this reason, the name Martha has always been associated with hard domestic work, as opposed to the contemplative life.

variants.name: Martha
meanings: New Testament name, of Aramaic rather than Hebrew origin, meaning ‘lady’. It was borne by the sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (John 11:1). According to Luke 10:38, when Jesus visited the house of Mary and Martha, Mary sat at his feet, listening to him, while Martha ‘was cumbered about much serving’, so that she complained to Jesus, ‘Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone?’ For this reason, the name Martha has always been associated with hard domestic work, as opposed to the contemplative life.

en.lifeSummary: When Marthe Roger de Beaufort was born in 1335, in Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, her father, Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT, was 41 and her mother, Marie de CHAMBON, was 42. She married Guy II de la Tour on 17 December 1353. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 6 September 1379, in Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, at the age of 44, and was buried in Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Marthe Roger de Beaufort est née en 1335, à Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Marie de CHAMBON, avait 42 ans. Elle a épousé Guy II de la Tour le 17 décembre 1353. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 6 septembre 1379, à Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Marthe Roger de Beaufort 1335 in Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT 41 und ihre Mutter Marie de CHAMBON 42. Sie hat am 17. Dezember 1353 Guy II de la Tour geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 6. September 1379 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich und wurde in Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Marthe Roger de Beaufort è nata il nel 1335 nel Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, sua padre, Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Marie de CHAMBON, aveva 42 anni. Lei sposato Guy II de la Tour il 17 dicembre 1353. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 6 settembre 1379, nel Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.

ja.Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes,: フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスロイアーモントブリソン
ja.Rosiers-d'Égletons, Ussel, Corrèze, Nouv: フランスノウベレ-アクイティアインコルeズロシエルス-ド'eグレトンス

ja.lifeSummary: Marthe Roger de Beaufortが1335年にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインコルeズロシエルス-ド'eグレトンスで生まれた時，父Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORTは41歳，母Marie de CHAMBONは42歳であった。1353年12月17日にGuy II de la Tourと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1379年9月6日にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスロイアーモントブリソンで44歳で死去し，フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスロイアーモントブリソンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Marthe Roger de Beaufort(이)가 1335년 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 코e즈 로시어스-드'e글레토스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie de CHAMBON(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 1353년 12월 17일 Guy II de la Tour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1379년 9월 6일 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 로이르 몽브리슨에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 로이르 몽브리슨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Marthe Roger de Beaufort nasceu em 1335, em Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Marie de CHAMBON, tinha 42 anos. Ela casou-se com Guy II de la Tour em 17 de dezembro de 1353. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 6 de setembro de 1379, em Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Marthe Roger de Beaufort родилась в 1335 г. в местности росиирс-д'eглетонс, коррeз, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Marie de CHAMBON, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy II de la Tour 17 декабря 1353 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 6 сентября 1379 г. в местности монтбрисон, лоир, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности монтбрисон, лоир, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Marthe Roger de Beaufort nació en 1335, en Rosiers-d'Égletons, Corrèze, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Guillaume II ROGIER de BEAUFORT, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Marie de CHAMBON, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Guy II de la Tour el 17 de diciembre de 1353. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 6 de septiembre de 1379, en Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultada en Montbrison, Loire, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.
